data.title: Denny's goes private as shareholders approve the buyout of 'America's Diner'
data.description:
Shareholders with Denny's (DENN) approved the restaurant company's $620M buyout by private equity investment company TriArtisan Capital Advisors (owner of TGI Fridays and P.F. Chang's), investment firm Treville Capital, and Yadav Enterprises, which is one of Denny's largest franchisees.

Looking back, Denny's (DENN) began in 1953 as a coffee shop concept that quickly shifted to a 24‑hour diner model and leaned heavily into franchising as it scaled through the 1960s and 1970s. The chain lived inside larger corporate structures and public vehicles for decades before eventually trading under its own name on the Nasdaq in 1997. Throughout the 2000s and 2010s, Denny's (DENN) management moved toward an asset‑light model, refranchising restaurants and positioning the stock as a cash‑generative, franchisor‑leaning diner brand with modest company‑owned exposure.​However, the restaurant stock lost more than 75% of its pre-pandemic high from 2019 in the challenging years that followed as the chain struggled with traffic headwinds, eventually leading the board to look at strategic alternatives.

As of June 25, 2025, the Denny's (DENN) store count stood at 1,558 restaurants, with 1,484 Denny’s‑brand locations and 74 Keke’s restaurants in the mix.

data.title: U.S. stocks slip as retail inflation comes in slightly cooler, earnings fail to lift sentiment
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages were lower on Tuesday as investors assessed core CPI inflation, which came in slightly cooler than consensus, and some important earnings reports.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last -0.2% in midday trade, while the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was flat, and the Dow (DJI) was -0.6%.

The December Consumer Price Index came in at +0.3% MoM vs. +0.3% consensus and +0.2% prior, +2.7% YoY vs. +2.6% consensus and +2.7% prior.

Core CPI (excludes food and energy) came in at +0.2% MoM vs. +0.3% consensus and +0.2% prior, +2.6% YoY vs. +2.7% consensus and +2.6% prior.

“Tuesday's CPI was in-line with expectations and that will likely keep the Federal Reserve on pause when it comes to interest rates for the foreseeable future… Recent positive employment data, elevated inflation, sticky price levels and political noise will keep the Fed at bay through at least the Spring,” said Skyler Weinand, CIO at Regan Capital.

“CPI is still under a cloud from the missing October number. Inflation is still relatively high, but trending downward. Consumers are still grappling with high overall price levels and sticker shock from the post-Covid jarring in housing, healthcare, education and everyday purchase prices,” Weinand added.

Also, new home sales slipped 0.1% MoM to 737K in October, vs. 716K expected, from 738K in the prior month, according to the U.S. Census Bureau.

Among companies reporting on Tuesday, JPMorgan Chase (JPM) -3.2% posted better-than-expected earnings, while Delta Air Lines (DAL) -2.5% had year-over-year revenue growth. 

Big companies reporting this week include Bank of America (BAC), Goldman Sachs (GS), and Citigroup (C).

Over in the bond market, the benchmark 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was 1 basis point higher to 4.19%, while the U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) was also unchanged at 3.54%. Investors are awaiting a 30-Year bond auction later this afternoon.

data.title: Mid to low cap industrial stocks with the strongest earnings momentum
data.description:
As the earnings season kicks off this week, we look at mid-to-low market cap industrial stocks with strong earnings momentum, which often attract investor attention as positive analyst revisions to earnings estimates can signal improving fundamentals.

The EPS Revision rating evaluates how analysts have adjusted their earnings per share estimates for a company over recent periods, with upgrades suggesting growing confidence in a company’s profitability outlook.

The following low to mid cap industrial sector stocks have earned the highest possible EPS revision rating of A+, indicating the most favorable trajectory of analyst earnings estimate changes.

The following industrial stocks have earned an A+ EPS revision grade:

  1. Arcosa (ACA) — EPS Revision Grade: A+
  2. Archer Aviation Inc. (ACHR) — EPS Revision Grade: A+
  3. AAR Corp. (AIR) — EPS Revision Grade: A+
  4. Atmus Filtration Technologies Inc. (ATMU) — EPS Revision Grade: A+
  5. Costamare Inc. (CMRE) — EPS Revision Grade: A+
  6. Eve Holding, Inc. (EVEX) — EPS Revision Grade: A+

  8. Exponent, Inc. (EXPO) — EPS Revision Grade: A+
  9. Flowserve Corporation (FLS) — EPS Revision Grade: A+
  10. Great Lakes Dredge & Dock Corporation (GLDD) — EPS Revision Grade: A+

Industrials ETFs: (XLI), (VIS), (FXR), (IYJ), (FIDU), and (RSPN)

data.title: Global PC shipments rose 9% in 2025; supply constraints cloud 2026 outlook
data.description:
Global shipments of desktops, notebooks and workstations rose 9.2% in 2025 to 279.5 million units, following a 10.1% increase in the fourth quarter, as per a report by Omdia.

Notebook shipments rose 8% in 2025 to 220.4 million units, while desktop shipments increased 14.4% to 59 million units. Omdia said tighter memory and storage supply emerged during the year, pushing costs higher and prompting vendors in December to signal price increases, which has already weighed on shipment expectations for 2026.

Between the first and fourth quarters of 2025, mainstream PC memory and storage costs rose 40% to 70%, Omdia said, adding that supply next year is unlikely to fully meet demand.

Lenovo (OTCPK:LNVGY) led the market in the fourth quarter and for the full year, shipping about 71 million units in 2025, up 14.6% from the last year.

HP (NYSE:HPQ) ranked second, while Dell  (NYSE:DELL) posted its strongest quarterly performance of 2025 with a 26% annual increase in fourth-quarter shipments.

Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) was the fastest-growing vendor for the full year, with shipments up 16.4% to about 28 million units, while Asus (OTCPK:ASUUY) completed the top five with around 20 million units shipped.

data.title: Dell added as a new short idea at Hedgeye
data.description:
Dell (DELL) was added as a new short idea at Hedgeye with the potential for downside to $100 a share.

"Memory inflation raises BOM just as DELL pushes PC price increases into a weak demand environment," Hedgeye analyst Felix Wang wrote in a note on Tuesday. “AI PC” optimism is not supported by real-world sentiment, raising downside risk to volumes and margins."

"NVIDIA’s accelerating vertical integration (VR200, NVL 576, Ultra) threatens OEM disintermediation," Wang added. "If neocloud demand slows, DELL’s AI backlog likely represents a near-term peak."

Consensus margins appear to be unrealistic, according to Wang. .

Dell (DELL) is likely to miss multiple October 2025 Investor Day targets, Wang wrote. EPS stagnation this year would drive multiple compression, implying fair value around $100.

Dell ticked lower by 0.6%. Shares of Dell have risen 9% over the past year.

data.title: Apple launches Creator Studio in new subscription package for digital creators
data.description:
Apple (AAPL) is launching Creator Studio in a push to entice digital creators, musicians, writers, students, educators and social media influencers to subscribe to a suite of tools in one subscription package. 

It includes Final Cut Pro, Logic Pro, Pixelmator Pro, Keynote, Pages, Numbers, and eventually Freeform. It can be accessed on Mac, iPad and iPhone. Furthermore, it will be available in the App Store on January 28 with a subscription price of $12.99 per month or $129 per year. College students and educators receive a substantial discount.

The package also features new artificial intelligence tools and features, such as Montage Maker in Final Cut Pro; access to Motion, which can create cinematic 2D and 3D effects; Synth Player, Chord ID, Sound Library and MainStage for music; and visual productivity improvements.

"Apple Creator Studio is a great value that enables creators of all types to pursue their craft and grow their skills by providing easy access to the most powerful and intuitive tools for video editing, music making, creative imaging, and visual productivity — all leveled up with advanced intelligent tools to augment and accelerate workflows," said Eddy Cue, Apple's senior vice president of Internet Software and Services.

data.title: Trump says soft inflation data mean deeper Fed rate cuts
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump again criticized Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell, arguing that the softer December inflation data underscored the need for more aggressive interest-rate reductions.

The core Consumer Price Index rose less than expected both M/M (+0.2%) and Y/Y (+2.6%) in 2025's last month, meaning that "Jerome 'Too Late' Powell should cut interest rates, MEANINGFULLY!!! If he doesn't he will just continue to be, 'TOO LATE!'

The Fed has already shaved the policy rate by 75 basis points last year, although expectations for this month's Federal Open Market Committee decision are largely for a pause, potentially snapping a three-meeting streak of cuts.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Bausch Health sets 2025 outlook at the higher end of the prior range
data.description:
• Ahead of its presentation at the J.P. Morgan Healthcare Conference this week, Bausch Health (BHC) on Tuesday set its full-year 2025 guidance at the higher end of the previously announced range, excluding its Bausch + Lomb (BLCO) subsidiary.  
  • In a regulatory filing, the Canadian pharma company said its management will issue several strategic and business updates at its JPM event scheduled to take place at 6:45 p.m. ET on January 14.
  • With its Q3 2025 financials, which beat Street forecasts in October, the Quebec-based company increased its full-year 2025 outlook for revenue and adjusted EBITDA to $10.05B-$10.25B and $3.57B-$3.66B, respectively.
  • Wall Street analysts currently expect Bausch Health (BHC) to report $3.91 of adjusted earnings per share on $10.18B in revenue for 2025.

data.title: Meta seeks to double output of Ray-Ban AI glasses -- report
data.description:
To capitalize on the growing demand for wearables, Meta (META) is reportedly in discussions with partner EssilorLuxottica (ESLOF) (ESLOY) to double production of AI-enabled Ray-Ban smart eyeglasses by the end of 2026.

According to Bloomberg, Meta (META) and EssilorLuxottica (ESLOF) (ESLOY) have discussed a production target of 20M units or more by the end of this year, with the potential to increase to more than 30M if needed.

EssilorLuxottica (ESLOF) (ESLOY) is currently producing 10M Ray-Ban glasses annually.

The ambitious production target underscores Meta’s (META) commitment to increase investment in artificial intelligence and the company’s confidence in the widespread adoption of smart glasses. Last week, the company said it was delaying the international expansion of Ray-Ban Display glasses due to “limited inventory” and “unprecedented demand,” while reports circulated on  Monday that Meta (META) was reducing its metaverse staff by 10% to reallocate assets to the teams developing augmented reality and artificial intelligence.

By ramping up production of smart glasses, Meta (META) hopes to expand its lead in the smart glasses market over rival Google (GOOG). Through a $150M investment in Warby Parker (WRBY), Google (GOOG) is developing intelligent glasses intended for all-day wear. The Android XR-powered glasses will be available this year and will include audio-focused glasses and display glasses.

While the news has done little to bolster Meta (META) shares, EssilorLuxottica (ESLOF) (ESLOY) and Warby Parker (WRBY) are both getting a lift with the former up 1.5% and the latter gaining more than 7% on speculation Google (GOOG) will match Meta’s (META) aggressive production goals.

data.title: Franklin Templeton broadens money market funds for stablecoins, blockchain
data.description:
Franklin Templeton (BEN) said Tuesday that two institutional money market funds managed by its affiliate Western Asset Management are now eligible for use in stablecoin reserve management and blockchain-enabled distribution markets.

Specifically, the update applies to two existing traditional Rule 2a-7 government money market funds -- the Western Asset Institutional Treasury Obligations Fund (LUIXX) and the Western Asset Institutional Treasury Reserves Fund (DIGXX).

The changes allow the funds to be used across two key segments of the emerging tokenized money market fund landscape, amid growing interest in digital delivery and tokenized access to traditional investment products.

“Traditional funds are already beginning to move on-chain, so rather than question their ability, our focus is to make them more accessible and useful by many,” said Roger Bayston, head of Digital Assets at Franklin Templeton. “By prioritizing interoperability and flexibility, we’re opening more ways for clients to access and deploy regulated funds across the platforms they rely on, giving investors greater choice in how they put their capital to work.”

A recent trend on Wall Street has been leveraging blockchain-based technologies to enhance traditional investment vehicles. BlackRock (BLK) and JPMorgan Chase (JPM) are among some of the big firms embarking on tokenized money market funds.
